map.condition: Computing average access time for a disk request
Finding total head movement for a disk scheduling algorithm
Computing average waiting time / turnaround time for a scheduling algorithm
Finding CPU efficiency lost to interrupt overhead
Computing bit rate of an I/O terminal

formulas.name: Total Disk Access Time
formulas.whenToUse: Estimating how long a single disk request takes end-to-end

terms.means: time to move head to the target track
time waiting for sector to rotate under the head
time to read/write the data once under head

formulas.trap: Average rotational latency is half the full rotation time (T_rot/2), not the full rotation time

formulas.name: Rotational Period and Average Latency
formulas.whenToUse: Converting RPM to rotational period and computing average wait for sector

terms.means: revolutions per minute of the disk
time for one full revolution

formulas.trap: RPM gives revolutions per minute; convert to seconds: T_rot = 60/RPM, not 1/RPM

formulas.name: Data Transfer Time
formulas.whenToUse: Computing time to read a known number of bytes from a rotating disk

terms.means: bytes stored on one full track
time for one full disk revolution

formulas.trap: Transfer time scales linearly with fraction of track; a full track read takes exactly T_rot

formulas.name: Disk Bandwidth / Transfer Rate
formulas.whenToUse: Estimating maximum sustained data rate of a disk

terms.means: bytes per track
disk rotation speed

formulas.trap: This is the peak rate; actual throughput is much lower due to seek and rotational latency

formulas.name: Total Head Movement (Any Algorithm)
formulas.plain: Total head movement = sum of |current_cylinder - next_cylinder| over all moves
formulas.whenToUse: Evaluating and comparing disk scheduling algorithms by seek distance

terms.means: cylinder number of the i-th request served
cylinder number of the previous position

formulas.trap: Always trace the order in which requests are served; SSTF and SCAN do not serve in FIFO order

formulas.name: SSTF — Shortest Seek Time First Rule
formulas.plain: Always serve the pending request closest to current head position
formulas.whenToUse: Simulating SSTF disk scheduling to find order served and total movement

terms.means: set of pending cylinder requests
current head cylinder position

formulas.trap: SSTF can cause starvation of far-away cylinders; it is NOT optimal in all cases

formulas.name: SCAN (Elevator) Algorithm
formulas.latex: \text{Head moves in one direction, services requests, reverses at end of disk}
formulas.plain: Head sweeps to one end, reverses direction, sweeps to other end; services requests along the way
formulas.whenToUse: SCAN / Elevator scheduling problems asking for service order or total movement

terms.means: current direction of head movement (increasing or decreasing cylinder)

formulas.trap: In SCAN the head reaches the last cylinder before reversing; C-SCAN wraps to cylinder 0 without servicing on the return trip

formulas.name: C-SCAN (Circular SCAN) Rule
formulas.latex: \text{One-direction sweep only; non-servicing jump from end back to start}
formulas.plain: Serve requests in one direction only; when end is reached, jump to other end without servicing
formulas.whenToUse: C-SCAN problems; provides more uniform wait times than SCAN

terms.means: non-servicing return from one end to the other

formulas.trap: The jump from end to start is NOT counted as head movement in many textbook problems; clarify convention

formulas.name: Turnaround Time and Waiting Time
formulas.latex: TT_i = C_i - A_i,\quad WT_i = TT_i - B_i
formulas.plain: TT_i = Completion_i - Arrival_i;  WT_i = TT_i - Burst_i
formulas.whenToUse: Computing individual and average turnaround/waiting times for any scheduling algorithm

terms.means: completion time of process i
arrival time of process i
CPU burst time of process i

formulas.trap: Waiting time = TT - burst (not TT - start time); response time = first scheduling time - arrival time

formulas.name: Average Waiting Time and Average Turnaround Time
formulas.whenToUse: Final step in any process scheduling problem to produce summary statistics

terms.means: total number of processes

formulas.trap: Count ALL processes; do not drop processes that arrive at time 0 or have zero wait

formulas.name: CPU Utilization
formulas.whenToUse: Measuring how efficiently the CPU is being used in a multiprogramming scenario

terms.means: time CPU is executing (not idle)

formulas.trap: Idle time (when all processes are in I/O) reduces utilization; count idle gaps carefully from the Gantt chart

formulas.name: Throughput
formulas.whenToUse: Evaluating scheduling algorithm by number of processes completed per unit time

terms.means: number of processes completed
total elapsed time

formulas.trap: Compare throughput of different algorithms over the same total time span for a fair comparison

formulas.name: SRTF (Preemptive SJF) Preemption Rule
formulas.plain: When a new process arrives, preempt current if new burst < remaining burst of running process
formulas.whenToUse: SRTF / preemptive SJF scheduling; deciding when to context switch

terms.means: burst time of the newly arrived process
remaining CPU time needed by the current running process

formulas.trap: Context switch happens only at arrival of a new process; the remaining burst shrinks continuously as the process runs

formulas.name: Round Robin — Context Switch Count
formulas.plain: Context switches ~ sum over all processes of ceil(B_i / q)
formulas.whenToUse: Round Robin problems asking how many context switches occur or comparing quantum sizes

terms.means: time quantum
CPU burst of process i

formulas.trap: Smaller quantum increases context switch overhead; larger quantum causes RR to behave like FCFS

formulas.name: Priority Scheduling — Starvation and Aging
formulas.plain: Aging: increase priority of waiting process by k per unit time to prevent starvation
formulas.whenToUse: Priority scheduling questions about starvation and its solution

terms.means: aging rate (priority increment per time unit waiting)

formulas.trap: Starvation can occur in SJF and static priority scheduling; Round Robin does NOT starve any process

formulas.name: CPU Time Available for Main Program
formulas.whenToUse: Finding what fraction of CPU time is spent on the main program vs. interrupt service routines

terms.means: time executing main program between interrupts
time to service one interrupt

formulas.trap: If interrupts arrive every X ms and ISR takes Y ms, efficiency = (X - Y)/X; ensure units match

formulas.name: Interrupt Cycle Components
formulas.plain: Interrupt cycle time = save_context + ISR_execution + restore_context
formulas.whenToUse: Detailed interrupt timing problems with context save/restore overhead specified

terms.means: time to push registers / PC onto stack
interrupt service routine execution time
time to pop and restore saved state

formulas.trap: Context save and restore add to interrupt overhead; omitting them underestimates total CPU time lost

formulas.name: I/O Terminal Bit Rate
formulas.whenToUse: Computing bit rate of a video terminal given characters per line, bits per char, and sweep time

terms.means: horizontal sweep time excluding retrace (blanking) time
number of characters per horizontal scan line

formulas.trap: Subtract the retrace (blanking) time from total sweep time to get the active data transmission window

formulas.name: Spooling — Definition and Key Property
formulas.plain: Spooling buffers output (e.g., print jobs) to disk so CPU is not blocked by slow device
formulas.whenToUse: I/O handling questions about which devices use spooling vs. direct I/O

terms.means: disk queue that decouples process output from slow I/O device

formulas.trap: An interactive terminal is NOT spooled; a shared printer IS the classic spooled device example
